Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT
WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to "worker registers with itself" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

THE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

OUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)

ABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.
- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.

CONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:
- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.
- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.
- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to "server-side preflight" decision in API design.
- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.
- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.
- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.

PARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):
- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.
- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.
- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.
- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.

SPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)

- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths
- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; "execution" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it
- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes
- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any
- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)
- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups
- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale

SEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads
- C5/C7/C9 explicitly FROZEN until the ADR's roadmap establishes the new package layout","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.009514865Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T021424-dac100b6","harness":"claude","input_tokens":21,"output_tokens":14799,"total_tokens":14820,"cost_usd":1.0767455,"duration_ms":249728,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.0358447Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=14820 cost_usd=1.0767"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:43.263235583Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"erik","body":"APPROVE

Linked from skills/ddx/SKILL.md intent-router.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:05:31.000159751Z","description":"Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — the domain reference file a harness reads when the user asks to create, plan, or update beads.

Content required:
- **Inline context, not links.** Every bead description must be readable cold by an automated execute-bead worker that has no chat history and no open tabs.
- **Explicit in-scope file list.** Repo-relative paths. Bad: 'the auth module'. Good: 'cli/internal/auth/pipeline.go and cli/internal/auth/pipeline_test.go'.
- **Explicit do-NOT-touch scope negation** (non-goals). Prevents scope creep during agent execution.
- **Favor many small beads.** Rule of thumb: if a bead would touch more than ~3 files or more than one subsystem, decompose into child beads under an epic.
- **Acceptance is a command that passes, not a sentence.** 'X works' is not AC. 'cd cli && go test ./internal/foo/... passes and returns 0' is AC.
- **Implementations include tests.** The acceptance criteria must reference a test command.
- **Rollback/cleanup expectations** for partial changes — what 'leave it unclaimed' means in practice.
- **spec-id linkage** when a governing artifact exists (FEAT-*, SD-*, TD-*, ADR-*); omit when it doesn't.
- **CLI reference** at the end: ddx bead create/update/close/list/show/ready/blocked/dep/import/export/evidence.

Source material: existing /skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md (already contains much of this — especially the top-level "Execution-Ready" section added in an earlier refinement). Strip HELIX-specific rules: remove the mandatory 'helix' label and the phase:frame|design|test|build|deploy|iterate|review enumeration. Keep generic guidance ('label beads with area, kind, and any workflow tags your project uses').

In-scope files:

.ddx/beads.jsonl:88:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.

2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.

3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.

4. **First-pass perf targets.**
   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.

5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**
   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.

6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.

7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.

8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"d4874cfd75778a35969c330285e33a126e359108","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:04.443214848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","depends_on_id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:21:53Z"}],"description":"## Observed

The Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be "an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model" — which is not what the current implementation does.

## Ground truth (current implementation)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —

- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.
- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.
- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.
- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.

## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)

- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: "how is harness/provider/model X doing?" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.
- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).
- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Reshape the data source

- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.
- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.
- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.
- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.

### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)

- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.
- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.
- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.

### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)

- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.

### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path

- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.
- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.

## Out of scope

- The `comparisons` / "compare arms" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).
- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).

This bead closes as 'investigation complete; see child beads for fixes'. ddx-b808df39 references this bead's findings in its implementation notes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:31:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"1d0b3e373a618623062909694332536c74f2630a","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:01:36.338860593Z","description":"## Context

ddx-b808df39 (P0: ddx agent stop/kill command for hung workers) treats hung-worker remediation as an operational need, but does NOT investigate the UNDERLYING cause of the hangs. The feature is a useful escape hatch, but shipping it without understanding WHY workers hang means we'll keep needing the escape hatch.

Opus fresh-eyes review (2026-04-18) flagged: 'Hung workers for 33h–142h are an operational defect independent of root cause' — the feature bead is justified, but a SIBLING root-cause investigation is missing from the portfolio.

## Observed

The referenced 33h and 142h hang durations in ddx-b808df39 imply multiple incidents where an execute-bead worker process remained in-progress past any reasonable upper bound for a single bead. Needs to investigate:

1. Is the worker process alive but stuck in a tool call (bash subprocess that never returns)?
2. Is it alive but mid-LLM-call with no timeout (provider hang + no client-side deadline)?
3. Is it dead but the bead's claimed_at never cleared (tracker-side leak)?
4. Is the worktree locked or fsync'd in a way that prevents cleanup?

## Fix direction

This bead is an INVESTIGATION, not a fix. Deliverable is a short report that:

1. Reproduces or observes at least one hang incident and captures:
   - ps / pgrep state of the worker process tree
   - stack traces via 'go tool pprof' against the worker (if alive)
   - Timeline from .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/*.jsonl (last event type + timestamp)
   - tracker claimed_at and retry_after state

2. Classifies the hang into one of the four categories above (or a new category).

3. Files concrete follow-up beads for each identified root cause:
   - Tool subprocess timeout if bash hangs
   - LLM client deadline if provider hangs
   - Claim expiry / heartbeat if tracker-side leak
   - File-lock / worktree cleanup if fs-side

4. Recommends whether ddx-b808df39's stop/kill mechanism is sufficient as mitigation or needs additional primitives.

## Relation to ddx-b808df39

ddx-b808df39 ships the OPERATOR TOOL to stop a hung worker. This bead finds the BUG that causes hangs. Both are needed; ship ddx-b808df39 first if the operator-tool is more urgent, then this investigation.

## Non-goals

- Fixing the root cause in this bead. Scope is investigation + follow-up bead filing.
- Preventing all possible hangs (impossible). Scope is the actually-observed hang No live hung worker was available\n+in this isolated worktree to capture live `ps`/`pprof`, so the incident evidence is\n+drawn from `.ddx/executions/*` bundles and the worker/runner source path.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 1. Observed evidence (from `.ddx/executions/`)\n+\n+| Metric                                               | Count / value                                        |\n+| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\n+| Total attempt bundles                                | 328                                                  |\n+| Bundles with `result.json`                           | 291 (89%)                                            |\n+| Bundles with `manifest.json` but **no** `result.json` | 37 (11%) — executions whose worker never finalized  |\n+\n+Top five longest *finalized* attempts:\n+\n+| `duration_ms` | Wall time    | `detail`                                                                    | Bundle                          |\n+| ------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\n+| 7,720,908     | **2h 8m**    | `agent: compaction stuck: 50 consecutive failed compaction attempts`        | 20260416T230316-efbf91c5        |\n+| 7,200,098     | **2h 0m**    | `timeout after 2h0m0s` (claude streaming wall-clock fired)                  | 20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba        |\n+| 6,764,927     | **1h 53m**   | success (agent harness, qwen)                                               | 20260416T230315-f9f46822        |\n+| 5,649,987     | **1h 34m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260416T230313-e193942b        |\n+| 4,284,347     | **1h 11m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260415T103422-bb3a45fa        |\n+\n+**Key observation:** the 33h / 142h hang durations cited in ddx-b808df39 are\n+~15× and ~65× longer than the longest *finalized* attempt on record. This means\n+the hung workers never reached the point of writing `result.json` — they were\n+blocked inside `runner.Run` (or deeper) for days. Every code path that\n+*should* have terminated them (idle timeout, compaction-stuck, stall detector,\n+cancel propagation) failed to fire.\n+\n+The 37 manifest-only bundles are candidates for hung or externally-killed\n+workers; e.g. `20260416T215953-6b515c05` has a manifest from 2026-04-16T21:59Z\n+with no result. Those fit the profile of \"workers running 33h–142h with no bead\n+assigned\" reported on agent projects.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\n+\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\n+\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\n+invocation**.\n+\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\n+\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\n+```go\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\n+}\n+```\n+\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\n+events.\n+\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\n+comment claims \"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\n+termination\" but the actual behaviour is **\"2 hours of silence required to fire\"**.\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\n+timer indefinitely.\n+\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\n+\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\n+\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\n+\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\n+\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\n+\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\n+read indefinitely.\n+\n+**Impact:** consistent with \"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\n+deadline\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. Cannot be fully confirmed\n+from source alone; needs runtime `go tool pprof http://localhost:<port>/debug/pprof/goroutine`\n+against a live hung worker.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — BashTool subprocess hang (hypothesis 1)\n+\n+Not the root cause. `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14/tool/bash.go:15`\n+installs a 120s default wall-clock timeout (`context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)`)\n+and calls `exec.CommandContext`. Per-call bash hangs self-terminate at 2min.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — Tracker-side claim leak (hypothesis 3)\n+\n+Tracker state is incidental to the hang: the bead's own description notes the\n+hung workers exist as processes (`ps aux | grep claude`). The worker process is\n+alive; the claim is alive because the worker is alive. Fixing the claim alone\n+does not kill the worker. This hypothesis is downstream, not upstream.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — Worktree file-lock (hypothesis 4)\n+\n+No evidence in bundles. Execute-bead worktrees are plain git worktrees at\n+`/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/*`; no process-held file locks would survive across 33–142h\n+without the owning process. If the process is alive, the lock is alive — same\n+as RC3.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. Relationship to ddx-b808df39\n+\n+ddx-b808df39 proposes an operator `ddx agent workers stop <id>` command that\n+sends **SIGTERM at the OS level**, then SIGKILL after a grace period. That\n+design **does** work around RC1 (process-level signal bypasses the broken ctx\n+plumbing). So ddx-b808df39 is a *correct mitigation* — but only a mitigation.\n+\n+Without fixing RC1 (and at least RC2), operators will keep needing to invoke\n+`workers stop` manually. The autonomous remediations every other system relies\n+on (cancel ctx, idle timeout, circuit breaker) are each broken today in ways\n+that require explicit code changes, not just a new operator tool.\n+\n+**Recommendation:** Ship ddx-b808df39 first (quick operational relief), then\n+resolve the child beads filed below in order RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. Follow-up beads filed\n+\n+Each addresses one root cause with a deterministic acceptance test. Priority\n+and ID are shown; full details available via `ddx bead show <id>`.\n+\n+The IDs below carry an `.execute-bead-wt-…` prefix because `ddx bead create`\n+was invoked from inside the isolated execute-bead worktree — this is a\n+separate DDx bug worth noting but orthogonal to the hang investigation. They\n+are parented to `ddx-0a651925` and resolvable by `ddx bead list --parent ddx-0a651925`.\n+\n+| RC  | Priority | Bead ID (suffix)                                                           | Title                                                              |\n+| --- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n+| RC1 | P0       | `…-526efaf1` | propagate caller context through Runner.Run and RunAgent           |\n+| RC2 | P0       | `…-b203c889` | add wall-clock deadline alongside idle timeout for agent harness   |\n+| RC3 | P2       | `…-b0562380` | bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock time                  |\n+| RC4 | P1       | `…-3e3913a0` | audit and enforce per-request HTTP deadlines on agent provider calls |\n+| —   | P1       | `…-34d039b7` | autonomous worker watchdog with process-level reaper (complements ddx-b808df39) |\n+\n+**Suggested execution order:** RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3 → watchdog. RC1 is a\n+prerequisite for RC4 being meaningful.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. What this investigation could NOT do\n+\n+- Capture live `ps`/`pprof` from a hung worker — none was accessible from the\n+  isolated execute-bead worktree. RC4 in particular needs a live `go tool pprof`\n+  goroutine dump to confirm that the stuck goroutine is in `net/http` streaming\n+  read, not some other path.\n+- Confirm whether the agent library's providers install any timeout that was\n+  missed in grep. Report at .ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md.

Root-cause summary:
- RC1 (PRIMARY): Runner.Run and Runner.RunAgent discard caller ctx (runner.go:239, agent_runner.go:163). WorkerManager.Stop cannot interrupt a running agent.
- RC2: idle-reset timer instead of wall-clock deadline for non-claude paths (executor.go:154, agent_runner.go:264).
- RC3: compaction-stuck breaker fires too late (~2h observed).
- RC4: provider HTTP calls likely lack per-request deadlines (confirm against live hung worker).
- BashTool subprocess hang ruled out (120s default timeout). Tracker-side / fs-lock hypotheses are downstream of RC1.

Follow-up beads filed (parent=ddx-0a651925):
- …-526efaf1 P0 [RC1] propagate caller context
- …-b203c889 P0 [RC2] wall-clock deadline
- …-3e3913a0 P1 [RC4] per-request HTTP deadlines
- …-b0562380 P2 [RC3] bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock
- …-34d039b7 P1 autonomous worker watchdog (complements ddx-b808df39)

ddx-b808df39's operator stop/kill is a correct mitigation but NOT sufficient as sole fix — until RC1 lands, operators will keep needing it. Ship ddx-b808df39 first, then RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3 → watchdog.

Today the single beads.jsonl carries the entire history (5.4MB and growing) and the events array on closed beads is the dominant size driver. This epic implements the architecture per ADR-004.

Decisions to make during implementation (carry over from this conversation):
- Archival trigger: file-size threshold (e.g. >4MB), age-based (closed >30d), or count-based (>500 closed). ADR-004 doesn't pick one.
- Whether closed-bead events arrays move into attachment storage (.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl) or stay inline in beads-archive.
- Migration semantics for the existing beads.jsonl (split in place vs explicit migrate command).

No server-side ReviewSession model, persistence layer, or schema exists. Story 18 review mutations and the Story 16 run-detail page both depend on a well-defined review session schema and persistence contract. Without this, both are blocked.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/ contains no ReviewSession struct or persistence layer.
- .ddx/reviews/ directory does not exist as a managed path (no mkdir, no lifecycle helpers).
- The Story 16 page (ddx-4cd64068) needs to read review turns from .ddx/reviews/<session-id>/turns.jsonl; the contract must be agreed before both 16 and 18 can land without merge conflicts.
- ddx-cd2ecf79 (artifact type spec, a dep) must land first for {template, review_prompt, preferred_reviewer} fields.

PROPOSED FIX
- Define ReviewSession struct (fields: id, artifact_id, artifact_sha, artifact_git_rev, system_rubric, template_ref, prompt_ref, status, turns[], cost_usd, max_billable_usd) in cli/internal/server/review_session.go.
- Implement persistence under .ddx/reviews/<session-id>/:
  - manifest.json: session metadata (id, status, cost, refs).
  - turns.jsonl: append-only JSONL of turn records (actor, content, cost_usd, created_at).
  - attachments/: binary attachments (if any).
- Document the manifest.json + turns.jsonl contract explicitly as the Story 16 contract.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL mutations (ddx-de278b8d).
- Turn dispatcher (ddx-f7c3d512).
- Frontend rendering.","id":"ddx-e7b80a50","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:18","area:server","kind:feature"],"parent":"ddx-42b917fe","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"review: server session schema/store/lifecycle + manifest+turns.jsonl evidence contract","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:24:52.797312144Z"}

Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T12:30:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"463338","closing_commit_sha":"3e58316310e9cf2122afed341ec0ca0a63b6f66d","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:35:10.781525398Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-ea76e1b8","depends_on_id":"ddx-a946c744","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:35:13Z"}],"description":"Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

Source material (consume in order):
1. /tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md — full integration plan with Axon's external interface mapped + GraphQL pivot + locked decisions
2. Cross-repo tracking bead axon-82b6f7b2 — full DDx-on-axon adoption checklist (links to all required axon-side capabilities)

LOCKED DECISIONS (do not relitigate; bake into TD):
1. Deployment: separate axon-server, operator-managed. DDx does NOT spawn or supervise Axon.
2. Auth: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. Axon already supports ts-net per its serve.rs (Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags). Reuses ADR-006.
3. Events storage: separate ddx_bead_events collection with event_of links (Option B). Two collections in Axon.
4. Offline posture: refuse all bead ops when Axon unreachable. No local cache, no WAL.
5. Wire transport: GraphQL only via genqlient-generated Go client. NOT gRPC. Subscriptions over WebSocket for live updates.

Cross-repo dependencies (tracked in axon-82b6f7b2):
- axon-05c1019d (P2): GraphQL pattern-query support — needed for single-query ddx bead ready/blocked. Day 1 ships with two-phase fallback.
- FEAT-013 secondary indexes (P1 Draft in axon): needed for status-field query performance at >1000 beads.
- FEAT-017 schema evolution (P1 Draft in axon): needed for clean DDx schema upgrades over time. Day 1 ships with DDx-side lazy migration on read.
- ts-net auth: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon. No coordination needed.
- GraphQL CRUD + subscriptions: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon.
- Multi-namespace/database: ALREADY SHIPPED.

Adoption gates (per axon-82b6f7b2):
- Alpha: requires axon-05c1019d + everything already-shipped. Acceptable to ship with table-scan perf.
- Beta: requires FEAT-013 (indexes) for >1000-bead performance.
- GA: requires FEAT-013 + FEAT-017 + axon-05c1019d all landed.

Defaults the TD can adopt without rechecking with operator (revisit only if implementation surfaces a problem):
- Schema versioning: DDx-side lazy migrate on read until FEAT-017 lands; switch to axon-native then.
- ddx bead ready Day 1: two-phase GraphQL query.
- Backup/DR: operator-managed via Axon's own backing-store DR.

NO CHANGES committed in this attempt.

## Why this bead is currently blocked

The bead description has four parts. Three of the four are blocked on a
prerequisite that has not landed:

(a) "Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands"
    (agent_list/capabilities/doctor/check/models/providers/route_status/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay)

(c) "Register DDx overrides on mounted subtree (deprecations + condense)"

Both presuppose a mounted upstream Cobra root grafted under `ddx agent`
— i.e. `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))` per FEAT-001-cli.md:69 and
plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md:28.

The CONTRACT-003 audit (committed 08a40b69) explicitly identifies this
as a gap (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md:23–89):

    > DDx cannot write `agentcli.NewRootCommand()` against the current
    > contract.
    >
    > The plan's "delete with prejudice" list (agent_list,
    > agent_capabilities, agent_doctor, agent_check, agent_models,
    > agent_providers, agent_route_status, agent_catalog,
    > agent_reindex) presupposes that upstream owns these subcommands
    > inside a mountable tree. Today they exist only as flag-driven
    > branches in cmd/agent/main.go that DDx cannot consume
    > programmatically.
    >
    > Plan bead #6 ("open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent`
    > if #5 surfaces gaps") is **required, not conditional**, because
    > of this gap.

Concretely:

  * cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 123–141) wires every soon-to-be-deleted
    file via `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCheckCommand())`,
    `newAgentModelsCommand()`, `newAgentProvidersCommand()`,
    `newAgentRouteStatusCommand()`, `newAgentListCommand()`,
    `newAgentCapabilitiesCommand()`, `newAgentDoctorCommand()`,
    `newAgentBenchmarkCommand()`, `newAgentCatalogCommand()`,
    `newAgentReplayCommand()`. Deleting any of those `agent_*.go`
    files without the upstream `MountCLI` replacement would leave
    agent_cmd.go referencing missing constructors and break the build.
  * The replacement (`cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`) requires
    the upstream module to expose `MountCLI` from a non-`internal/`
    package, which the audit specifies as the upstream amendment
    CONTRACT-003-A (port `cmd/agent/main.go` from `flag` to `cobra`,
    move into `agent/cli`, re-export `MountCLI`, bump module version).
    That work has not happened in `~/Projects/agent`.

(b) "Migrate DDx-only subcommands"
    (agent_executions → tries_*; agent_log → runs_log;
     agent_metrics → runs_metrics; agent_usage folded into runs_metrics;
     agent_workers → work_workers)

This portion is mechanically possible inside DDx alone, but per the
plan (line 208) beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically — with #15
landing in isolation, the new namespaces (`ddx runs`, `ddx tries`,
`ddx work workers`) need top-level registration that has no analogue
yet in command_factory_commands.go. The implementation P2 phase of the
plan (lines 196–204) lists "CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work
workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)" as separate work
that comes after the frame/design beads. Doing only (b) here, without
(a) and (c), produces a confusing CLI surface where the same evidence
is reachable both as `ddx agent executions` and `ddx tries list`, and
it strands the deprecation messages that need to point at the new
verbs.

(d) "Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    new verbs"

This depends on the new verbs (`ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx runs *`,
`ddx tries *`, `ddx work workers *`) actually existing as runnable
commands so that the updated scripts/skills run green. `ddx run` and
`ddx try` are not registered today (grep for `Use:.*\"run\"` /
`Use:.*\"try\"` against cli/cmd/*.go shows only the `ddx agent run`
inner command). Updating scripts/eval-skill.sh,
scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh, scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh,
scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md, etc. to call
non-existent verbs would break those flows immediately and would
specifically break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced
this very plan (plan line 208).

## What an unblocked attempt needs

Roughly in dependency order:

1. Upstream amendment CONTRACT-003-A merged in `~/Projects/agent`:
   port to cobra, move commands to `agent/cli`, export `MountCLI`,
   tag a release, and bump `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` in
   DDx's go.mod (no replace directive — see memory
   feedback_no_local_replace).
2. Top-level `ddx run` and `ddx try` registered as first-class
   commands under CommandFactory (the implementation P2 thread of the
   plan).
3. `ddx runs`, `ddx tries`, `ddx work workers` namespace skeletons
   registered, even if their subcommand implementations are then
   ported in this bead.

Once those are in, the present bead becomes:

  * Replace the 11+ enumerated `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgent*Command())`
    calls in agent_cmd.go with `cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`,
    plus deprecation override registrations for `agent run`,
    `agent execute-bead`, `agent execute-loop`, and the override for
    `agent condense` (DDx-specific filter).
  * `git rm` agent_check.go, agent_models.go, agent_providers.go,
    agent_route_status.go (the only "delete with prejudice" files
    that actually exist in DDx today; agent_list/capabilities/doctor/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay only exist as inline functions
    inside agent_cmd.go and would be removed by the agent_cmd.go
    rewrite).
  * Move agent_executions.go → tries_*.go; agent_metrics.go →
    runs_metrics.go (folding agent_usage.go into it);
    agent_workers.go → work_workers.go; rename `newAgentLogCommand`
    in agent_cmd.go into `runs_log.go`. Update tests in lockstep.
  * Sweep scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    swap old verbs for new (e.g. `ddx agent run` → `ddx run`,

rationale: Bead ddx-ebf3c09b — repo-wide verb migration + agent_*.go cleanup
===================================================================

NO CHANGES committed in this attempt.

## Why this bead is currently blocked

The bead description has four parts. Three of the four are blocked on a
prerequisite that has not landed:

(a) "Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands"
    (agent_list/capabilities/doctor/check/models/providers/route_status/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay)

(c) "Register DDx overrides on mounted subtree (deprecations + condense)"

Both presuppose a mounted upstream Cobra root grafted under `ddx agent`
— i.e. `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))` per FEAT-001-cli.md:69 and
plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md:28.

The CONTRACT-003 audit (committed 08a40b69) explicitly identifies this
as a gap (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md:23–89):

    > DDx cannot write `agentcli.NewRootCommand()` against the current
    > contract.
    >
    > The plan's "delete with prejudice" list (agent_list,
    > agent_capabilities, agent_doctor, agent_check, agent_models,
    > agent_providers, agent_route_status, agent_catalog,
    > agent_reindex) presupposes that upstream owns these subcommands
    > inside a mountable tree. Today they exist only as flag-driven
    > branches in cmd/agent/main.go that DDx cannot consume
    > programmatically.
    >
    > Plan bead #6 ("open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent`
    > if #5 surfaces gaps") is **required, not conditional**, because
    > of this gap.

Concretely:

  * cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 123–141) wires every soon-to-be-deleted
    file via `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCheckCommand())`,
    `newAgentModelsCommand()`, `newAgentProvidersCommand()`,
    `newAgentRouteStatusCommand()`, `newAgentListCommand()`,
    `newAgentCapabilitiesCommand()`, `newAgentDoctorCommand()`,
    `newAgentBenchmarkCommand()`, `newAgentCatalogCommand()`,
    `newAgentReplayCommand()`. Deleting any of those `agent_*.go`
    files without the upstream `MountCLI` replacement would leave
    agent_cmd.go referencing missing constructors and break the build.
  * The replacement (`cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`) requires
    the upstream module to expose `MountCLI` from a non-`internal/`
    package, which the audit specifies as the upstream amendment
    CONTRACT-003-A (port `cmd/agent/main.go` from `flag` to `cobra`,
    move into `agent/cli`, re-export `MountCLI`, bump module version).
    That work has not happened in `~/Projects/agent`.

(b) "Migrate DDx-only subcommands"
    (agent_executions → tries_*; agent_log → runs_log;
     agent_metrics → runs_metrics; agent_usage folded into runs_metrics;
     agent_workers → work_workers)

This portion is mechanically possible inside DDx alone, but per the
plan (line 208) beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically — with #15
landing in isolation, the new namespaces (`ddx runs`, `ddx tries`,
`ddx work workers`) need top-level registration that has no analogue
yet in command_factory_commands.go. The implementation P2 phase of the
plan (lines 196–204) lists "CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work
workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)" as separate work
that comes after the frame/design beads. Doing only (b) here, without
(a) and (c), produces a confusing CLI surface where the same evidence
is reachable both as `ddx agent executions` and `ddx tries list`, and
it strands the deprecation messages that need to point at the new
verbs.

(d) "Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    new verbs"

This depends on the new verbs (`ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx runs *`,
`ddx tries *`, `ddx work workers *`) actually existing as runnable
commands so that the updated scripts/skills run green. `ddx run` and
`ddx try` are not registered today (grep for `Use:.*\"run\"` /
`Use:.*\"try\"` against cli/cmd/*.go shows only the `ddx agent run`
inner command). Updating scripts/eval-skill.sh,
scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh, scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh,
scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md, etc. to call
non-existent verbs would break those flows immediately and would
specifically break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced
this very plan (plan line 208).

## What an unblocked attempt needs

Roughly in dependency order:

1. Upstream amendment CONTRACT-003-A merged in `~/Projects/agent`:
   port to cobra, move commands to `agent/cli`, export `MountCLI`,
   tag a release, and bump `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` in
   DDx's go.mod (no replace directive — see memory
   feedback_no_local_replace).
2. Top-level `ddx run` and `ddx try` registered as first-class
   commands under CommandFactory (the implementation P2 thread of the
   plan).
3. `ddx runs`, `ddx tries`, `ddx work workers` namespace skeletons
   registered, even if their subcommand implementations are then
   ported in this bead.

Once those are in, the present bead becomes:

  * Replace the 11+ enumerated `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgent*Command())`
    calls in agent_cmd.go with `cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`,
    plus deprecation override registrations for `agent run`,
    `agent execute-bead`, `agent execute-loop`, and the override for
    `agent condense` (DDx-specific filter).
  * `git rm` agent_check.go, agent_models.go, agent_providers.go,
    agent_route_status.go (the only "delete with prejudice" files
    that actually exist in DDx today; agent_list/capabilities/doctor/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay only exist as inline functions
    inside agent_cmd.go and would be removed by the agent_cmd.go
    rewrite).
  * Move agent_executions.go → tries_*.go; agent_metrics.go →
    runs_metrics.go (folding agent_usage.go into it);
    agent_workers.go → work_workers.go; rename `newAgentLogCommand`
    in agent_cmd.go into `runs_log.go`. Update tests in lockstep.
  * Sweep scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    swap old verbs for new (e.g. `ddx agent run` → `ddx run`,
    `ddx agent execute-loop` → `ddx work`,
    `ddx agent execute-bead` → `ddx try`,
    `ddx agent log/metrics/usage/executions/workers` → the new
    `runs/tries/work workers` paths). Specific known references:
    scripts/eval-skill.sh, scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh,
    scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh, scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
    library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md,
    library/personas/README.md, cli/cmd/init.go:757,
    cli/cmd/bead_review.go:30–33, plus a wider grep across
    docs/**/*.md and library/**/*.md.

ADR-022 step 7 requires migrating from in-process agent execution to a server-spawn model (workers spawned by the server, not by the CLI directly). The current execute-loop launch path uses cli/internal/server/workers.go handleStartExecuteLoopWorker to start a worker subprocess, but this handler may not properly validate all execution parameters or follow the unified ExecuteLoopSpec lifecycle (ApplyDefaults → Validate → StartExecuteLoop).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/workers.go handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (exact line to be confirmed after ddx-89a9c305 lands): the current server-spawn path constructs CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime manually rather than going through ApplyDefaults → Validate → StartExecuteLoop with a unified spec.
- triage:needs-investigation label was added because the exact wiring was unclear at filing time. Investigation findings: the server-spawn handler calls StartExecuteLoop via the workers subsystem, but intermediate struct construction (before ddx-89a9c305 unifies the spec) may drop fields.
- The server binary is the one that actually execs 'ddx work'; the subprocess inherits from server-resolved settings, not from a fully validated ExecuteLoopSpec.

PROPOSED FIX
- Confirm handleStartExecuteLoopWorker in cli/internal/server/workers.go validates through ApplyDefaults → Validate before calling StartExecuteLoop.
- If gaps exist: wire the unified ExecuteLoopSpec (from ddx-89a9c305) into the spawn path.
- Verify the spawned 'ddx work' subprocess receives all spec fields (MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, FromRev) via the exec args or a spec file.

NON-SCOPE
- Changes to the exec'd ddx work subprocess behavior (that's C10 / ddx-f9acb86d).

Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package. ### Actual surface (vs stated estimate) cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together: - in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction - per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization - in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` - in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation) - in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService) - per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net - progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` - watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS - terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result - BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via: - /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress - PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial) - worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging - Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent ### Test surface cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either: - a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or - deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface). This is the bulk of the work, not "test updates." ### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end) "server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit" Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper. ### Size-check verdict - More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables. - AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? reason: Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs.

## Why this attempt declined to commit

The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package.

### Actual surface (vs stated estimate)

cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together:

- in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction
- per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization
- in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer`
- in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation)
- in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService)
- per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net
- progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress`
- watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS
- terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result
- BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes

Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via:

- /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress
- PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial)
- worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging
- Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent

### Test surface

cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either:

- a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or
- deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface).

This is the bulk of the work, not "test updates."

### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end)

"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit"

Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper.

### Size-check verdict

- More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables.
- AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate).
- More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes.

The bead's "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work.

## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo)

Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable):

### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing
Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID)
Scope:
- Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env).
- Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works.
- Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl.
- Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess.
- Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path.
- Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection.
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.
Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green.
Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC.

### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion
Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec
Scope:
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct.
- Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling.
- Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal.
Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green.
Estimated: ~300–500 LOC.

### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude)
Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit
Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires:
- Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port.
- Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead.
- POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once.
- Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status).
- Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH.
Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented.
Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra.

### Dependencies
- 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec)
- 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape)

## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit

The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree.

## Bookkeeping

A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. (a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------
The previous attempt (commit 5ae02964) migrated the high-value `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
and `RunViaService(..., RunOptions{...})` test sites onto the SD-024 successor API
(`r.RunWithConfig` + `RunWithConfigViaService` + `AgentRunRuntime`) across:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go    (~22 sites: TestRunWith*, TestSessionLogging,
                                         TestRunPromptFile, TestRunStdinMode,
                                         TestRunModel*, TestRunNonZeroExit,
                                         TestRunUnknownHarness, TestRunEmptyPrompt,
                                         TestIntegration_CodexEcho, TestRunOpencode*,
                                         and the *Permissions* pair)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go   (lmstudio / openrouter dispatch regressions)
  - cli/internal/agent/script_test.go   (runScript helper now builds rcfg via
                                         TestRunConfigOpts and dispatches via
                                         RunWithConfigViaService)

Those 26 sites are the bulk of the "test sites that exercise the high-level agent
run path" called out in the bead description.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------
The bead AC reads:

    rg 'RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{' cli/ returns 0 matches outside
    the type definitions about to be retired.

This is a *global* gate. It cannot be satisfied by test-only changes — it requires
retiring every remaining `RunOptions{...}` construction in both production and test
code. The remaining sites fall into two classes:

CLASS A — tests that call functions whose signature *takes* RunOptions, so the
RunOptions construction is not a "test site" the test could rewrite without
changing the function under test:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites
                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go
        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt
        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper
        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)
        line 278 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/models_test.go
        line 45  BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go
        lines 164/174/214 runVirtualFn(runner, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go
        lines 206/212/218/224/291/324  internal opts: RunOptions{...} fixtures

Migrating any of these test sites requires first changing the *signature* of the
production function under test (BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
RunViaServiceWith, runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, SessionIndexEntryFromResult,
plus the internal claude_stream helpers). That is production-code refactor work,
explicitly outside the scope the bead description names.

CLASS B — production callers still constructing RunOptions{...}:

  - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go              :1462
  - cli/internal/server/server.go     :1913, :3786
  - cli/internal/exec/store.go        :201
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go      :248, :281, :300, :321, :976
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go :82  (RunWithConfigViaService bridge — by design)
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go :61, :261, :414, :685
  - cli/internal/agent/grade.go       :169
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go :706
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go :531

Until these are migrated (and in many cases until the function signatures they
flow through are migrated), the AC's grep gate cannot return zero matches.

The fresh-eyes review on the prior attempt itself concluded:

    Path forward: simplest fix is splitting follow-up beads for
    (a) prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
    (b) remaining *_test.go RunOptions sites,
    (c) production-code migration,
    then closing this bead with a narrower AC.

That assessment is correct. The bead title ("5-10 sites") and description ("migrate
test sites … in agent_test.go and any sibling test using RunOptions") describe a
test-scoped refactor; the AC describes a global retirement. The two are not
compatible inside one bead.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------
Either:

  Option 1 (preferred): re-cut this bead with a narrowed AC, e.g.

      "rg 'RunOptions\{' cli/internal/agent/{agent,runner,script}_test.go returns
      0 matches in tests that exercise r.Run / RunViaService."

      That AC matches the test-only scope, and the prior commit already satisfies it.

  Option 2: expand bead scope to cover the production migration as well. That is
      a multi-file Stage-2 refactor touching cmd/, internal/server/, internal/exec/,
      and most of internal/agent/. It also requires migrating the signatures of
      BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunViaServiceWith,
      runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and SessionIndexEntryFromResult so the
      test sites in Class A can shed RunOptions. This belongs in its own bead (or
      epic), not in a "5-10 sites" test-migration bead.

No new code is committed in this attempt because:

  1. The work the bead description actually scopes is already done (5ae02964).
  2. Doing test-only edits that don't move the AC gate would be exploratory churn.
  3. Doing the full production migration here would silently expand the bead scope
     past what the description and title authorize.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1108:{"acceptance":"1. cli/cmd/try.go exists implementing 'ddx try <bead-id>' as a cobra subcommand. Help text describes operator-targeted execution + the relationship to 'ddx work'.

2. 'ddx try unknown-bead-id' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead not found: unknown-bead-id'.

3. 'ddx try <closed-bead-id>' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead is not claimable (status=closed)'. Same shape for cancelled.

4. 'ddx try <bead-id-with-unmet-deps>' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead has unmet dependencies: <list>'.

5. 'ddx try <ready-bead-id>' claims the bead, executes via the existing execute-bead-loop machinery (one iteration, one bead), writes evidence to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/, and exits with the bead's terminal status code (0 = success/already_satisfied; 1 = preserved_for_review/no_changes; 2 = execution_failed).

6. Flag plumbing: --harness, --model, --profile, --provider, --effort, --opaque-passthrough, --no-review, --review-harness, --review-model. Each flag, when set, plumbs identically to the corresponding 'ddx work' flag (same struct fields in the runtime config).

7. WIRED-IN: the new 'ddx try' command is registered in cli/cmd/root.go (or wherever the cobra root command assembles subcommands). Verified by 'ddx try --help' output AND by 'git grep "tryCmd\|NewTryCommand" cli/cmd/' showing the registration.

8. Tests in cli/cmd/try_test.go:
   - TestTry_BeadNotFound — invalid ID returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit
   - TestTry_BeadClosed — closed bead returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit
   - TestTry_BeadUnmetDeps — bead with unclosed dep returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit
   - TestTry_HappyPath_ClaimsAndExecutes — fixture bead + stub executor; assert bead is claimed, attempted, and outcome reported (use the existing test fixtures from agent_execute_loop_test.go as a template)
   - TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough — set --harness=codex --model=gpt-5.4-mini and assert these reach the runtime config (capture via stub)

9. Quality gates:
   - cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/...
   - lefthook run pre-commit

10. Conventional commit message ending [<this-bead-id>]. Stage cli/cmd/try.go + cli/cmd/try_test.go + any cli/cmd/root.go registration line specifically; DO NOT use git add -A.

11. Manual verification (operator runs after merge): 'ddx try ddx-50da9674' (closed fixture-repo bead) returns non-zero with the closed-bead message; 'ddx try <some-ready-bead>' actually claims and executes it.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T20:06:45Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"463338","closing_commit_sha":"ff5e64cecc17f3094f3933986966b94a50dc4f59","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:06:37.62309311Z","description":"PROBLEM
Operators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:
- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead
- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled
- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log

This blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.

ROOT CAUSE
'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.

Code today (relevant entry points):
- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:

  ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>]

Behavior:
1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.
2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.
3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):
   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.
   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.
   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.
4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).
5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).

NON-SCOPE
- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)
- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)
- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)
- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)
- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)

INTERSECTION
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.
- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.
- Refactor children Wave 2 dispatch will use 'ddx try' (codex harness) to deterministically dispatch each child in dep order.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:33:19.857924528Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T200645-f1713f26","harness":"claude","input_tokens":112,"output_tokens":52083,"total_tokens":52195,"cost_usd":8.31247625,"duration_ms":1590247,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:33:19.956008288Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=52195 cost_usd=8.3125"},{"actor":"erik","body":"preserve_ref=refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-f261d2a0/20260503T200645-f1713f26-88dde5dc9001

2026-05-04 02:24:15: 'ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex' returned no_changes after one attempt. This is strong evidence for the P7 (bead-as-prompt) hypothesis from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES (ddx-06b77652): bead authoring quality is a dominant factor in execution success, possibly more dominant than the execution machinery itself.

CONTRAST
- Non-retrofit ddx-29058e2a (multi-file scope, 'see also' references, no concrete starting point): no_changes
- Retrofit ddx-aee651ec (concrete file paths, named types): success at commit ?? (closed 2026-05-03 20:46)
- Retrofit ddx-3e60fd84 (file:line cites, named tests): success at commit ?? (closed 2026-05-03 22:24)

CONCLUSION
The 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path B-vs-C question (sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated) has a partial answer already: when bead is well-authored, sub-agent + verbatim bead succeeds; when bead is poorly authored, even capable executors return no_changes.
