Review area: embedded ddx-agent runtime observability and live worker progress. Evidence covers FEAT-006 execution/evidence ownership, FEAT-002 server activity surfaces, FEAT-008 web UI expectations, execute-loop worker supervision, and the need for DDx-owned progress telemetry beyond coarse session logs when DDx runs the embedded harness.
</context-digest>
Define the progress-event contract for managed embedded-agent runs so DDx can surface meaningful live execution status in the CLI and server/UI.

## Goals
- Define structured phase/heartbeat output for embedded-agent runs (queueing, launch, running, post-checks, landing, preserved, done)
- Define the minimal per-event fields needed for operator visibility and later reporting: worker/project/bead/run identity, phase, elapsed/heartbeat timing, harness/provider/model/profile, and token/cost deltas when available
- Clarify which live progress artifacts remain runtime-only and which summaries/indexes are exposed through DDx-owned APIs and UI surfaces
- Keep provider-native transcript ownership external; DDx progress output should be normalized runtime telemetry, not transcript duplication

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-006, FEAT-002, and FEAT-008 so embedded-agent progress visibility is an explicit contract rather than an implementation detail
- Clarify how progress events relate to tracked execution artifacts, session logs, and server-managed worker state

## Required implementation planning
- Define the server/API/UI read model for live worker progress and recent phase history
- Define how CLI and UI consume the same structured progress feed without introducing hidden workflow policy","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"checkpointing dirty worktree: git stash: error: could not write index

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1127:{"acceptance":"AC1. `ddx agent workers prune` (or equivalent) exists and reaps registry entries whose recorded PID is no longer alive (or whose started_at is older than a configurable threshold). Output lists each reaped worker with id, bead_id, harness, age.

AC2. `ddx agent workers stop --bead <id>` succeeds against a stale registry entry too — it should release the registry slot even when the underlying process has already exited. Current 'no matching workers' / 'worker not running' contradiction must be resolved.

AC3. The local-worker registry persists or is reconstructed in a way that survives ddx-server restarts cleanly: either persisted with PID liveness check on restart, or rebuilt from execute-bead/execute-loop on-disk evidence (.ddx/executions/<id>/result.json).

AC4. `ddx agent workers --json` includes a 'liveness' or 'pid_alive' field per entry so operators can see at a glance which entries are live vs stale, even before pruning.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-29T17:59:55Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"932496","closing_commit_sha":"07ae8022f9f55ba3fecc2378ae28e2e0eabf0cad","created_at":"2026-04-29T13:14:56.008884527Z","description":"When a local execute-bead/execute-loop process exits abnormally (killed, OOM, parent shell terminated, container crash) the worker entry can remain in the in-memory registry forever. `ddx agent workers` lists it as state=running with elapsed_ms continuously advancing, even though no PID exists.

Reproducing today (axon repo): worker local-20260429T030828-075a28c5 (bead hx-5f4d4ea1, harness=agent, model=qwen3.6) started at 03:08 UTC, has logged elapsed=10+ hours, and `ps -ef | grep execute-bead` shows nothing. The git worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-hx-5f4d4ea1-* has only the checkpoint commit (no actual work commits), confirming the worker died early.

Available paths to clean up are all unsatisfying:
1. `ddx agent workers stop --bead hx-5f4d4ea1` returns 'no matching workers' (because the matcher only finds live workers)
2. `ddx agent workers stop <worker-id>` returns server error 400 'worker not running' (same contradiction — the registry says running, the stopper says not running)
3. `systemctl --user restart ddx-server` works but kills every other active worker on the box, including ones for other repos that are doing real work
4. Manual edit of server-state.json risks corruption and isn't documented

The bead itself wasn't blocked (status=open, owner=null) so the registry noise is mostly cosmetic — it pollutes `ddx agent workers` output and confuses operators trying to triage the queue. But over weeks of use these accumulate (saw 14+ stale entries from prior sessions on this same box, with ages 134h–268h).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"sonnet","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:16:17.989012103Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260429T175957-601d3ad4","harness":"claude","model":"sonnet","input_tokens":61,"output_tokens":44775,"total_tokens":44836,"cost_usd":3.025082300000001,"duration_ms":979503,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:16:18.100205295Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=44836 cost_usd=3.0251 model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"sonnet","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:16:22.364943799Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"APPROVE

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1130:{"acceptance":"(1) A regression test at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go (or execute_bead_land_test.go) runs a simulated successful execute-bead attempt against a real temp git repo and asserts: (a) after the run returns, the working tree is clean (git status --porcelain is empty), and (b) at least one file under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ is present in git log --all --oneline for the attempt's result or successor commit. Test must fail on current HEAD (98e379b) and pass post-fix. (2) The same test uses bead tracker evidence to confirm the bead's closing_commit_sha still resolves after the merge (git cat-file -e). (3) cli/internal/server/workers_test.go TestWorkerManagerStartAndShow (or a sibling test) exercises the server-worker path and asserts the same clean-worktree guarantee. (4) Full test suite (cli: go test ./cmd ./internal/agent ./internal/server) passes. (5) Manual dogfooding: ddx agent execute-loop --once against a ready bead leaves git status --porcelain empty, and HEAD or HEAD^ contains at least one file under .ddx/executions/<attempt>/. (6) No changes to AGENTS.md, FEAT-006, or skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md — the policy is already correct; this is implementation only.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"ce25dda3c76977d7d66e0a39dc6e93b0f2b25183","created_at":"2026-04-15T15:34:35.535203989Z","description":"## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.
- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,
  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit
  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.

## Cross-Bead Context

- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` ("Evolve execute-bead into a tracked
  execution-evidence workflow", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this
  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.
- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

## Verification Commands

- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`
  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"agent","route_reason":"first-available","fallback_chain":[],"base_url":"http://vidar:1234/v1"}","created_at":"2026-04-16T04:47:09.142456953Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=agent reason=first-available"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"agent","route_reason":"first-available","fallback_chain":[],"base_url":"http://bragi:1234/v1"}","created_at":"2026-04-16T18:11:14.003181123Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=agent reason=first-available"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"claude-opus-4-6","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-16T18:34:21.429059193Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=claude-opus-4-6"}],"id":"ddx-f7ac7826","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:exec","area:git"],"parent":"ddx-32b3008e","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"orchestrator: land execution evidence and leave worktree clean after agent run","updated_at":"2026-04-16T18:34:37.770774687Z"}

If the agent module does not yet expose a normalized final-response API, this bead is blocked on the agent-side stream normalizer bead (sibling in ~/Projects/agent).
(2) Fixture reviewer-stream-approve-misextracted.jsonl (from ddx-004eccbc) drives a regression test: the pre-fix behavior records BLOCK; the fix records APPROVE.
(3) ParseReviewVerdict no longer defaults to BLOCK on an unrecognized verdict line. Unrecognized output returns a parse error; the caller handles that as a review-error (feeds ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path), not a silent BLOCK.
(4) Unit tests cover: clean APPROVE, clean BLOCK, REQUEST_CHANGES, unparseable, empty.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:55:04.187171368Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-f7ae036f","depends_on_id":"ddx-004eccbc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:04:11Z"}],"description":"## Wave 2 framing: risk-reduction, not correctness

This bead (scope:partial) stops silent-BLOCK mis-records — it does NOT stop mis-extraction itself. After wave:2 lands, the reviewer will still misfire on codex/claude raw stream frames because clause (1) (consume normalized final text) is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5. What changes: misfires surface as loud review-error reopens instead of silent BLOCKs, feeding ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path. Expect a "review-error reopens per closed bead" metric to look rough in this window. The real extraction fix lands when agent-32e8ff5e ships upstream and ddx-7bc0c8d5 migrates.

---

Reporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 1 of review-malfunction report.

The post-merge reviewer in `ddx agent execute-loop` records `{"verdict":"BLOCK"}` in `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/result.json` even when the reviewer LLM produced a well-formed `### Verdict: APPROVE` (or REQUEST_CHANGES) with full AC grade table and cited evidence. Observed ~20 times in a row on nexiq codex+claude loop 2026-04-19 against beads nexiq-b5661a77, nexiq-50485cd3, nexiq-be1bc61d, nexiq-8999acab, hx-8a26c6df, hx-4901d83a, hx-e33210f6, nexiq-35d64a6b, and more — every bead in the run.

Code site: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:76` — `reVerdictLine` regex `^#{1,4}\s+Verdict:\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\s*$`. When the reviewer output contains the raw verdict on a heading line but is wrapped inside a codex streaming JSON event body (e.g. `{"type":"item.completed","item":{..."text":"### Verdict: APPROVE..."}}` with escaped newlines), the regex does not match across the wrapped payload and `ParseReviewVerdict` falls through to the `VerdictBlock` default at line 86 ("safe default: requires human attention"). The bead then gets a `review_malfunction` event with detail "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)".

Actual reviewer output is preserved in `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` under the reviewer session id and can be recovered post-facto; the parser just fails to see it.

Hypothesis from report: parser anchors on `### Verdict:` heading but a codex harness stream serializes text with `\n` escaping or wraps the reviewer body inside thread/turn JSON events so the multiline anchor never hits. Root fix options: (a) extract reviewer text from the raw stream before parsing (strip codex thread/turn JSON to the inner `item_0.text` payload); (b) switch the reviewer prompt to require machine-readable JSON `{verdict, ac_grades, summary}` and parse that; (c) broaden the regex to match non-anchored `### Verdict: X` headings even inside JSON-escaped text.

## Upstream dependency

Blocked on agent-32e8ff5e (in ~/Projects/agent): the fix moves reviewer-text extraction behind the ddx-agent service boundary so DDx no longer parses raw harness stream frames. Until that lands, f7ae036f's clause (1) on "normalized reviewer text" cannot be satisfied and the regression test from ddx-004eccbc remains red. The parse-error / no-default-BLOCK changes (clauses 3-4) can land independently and are worth doing now.

## Scope split (wave 2)

Clauses (3) and (4) — no default-BLOCK, parse errors surface as review-error, fixture regression test — land in wave 2 with only ddx-004eccbc as a prereq.

Clause (1) — consume normalized final text from the agent service instead of raw stream frames — is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5 because it depends on agent-32e8ff5e shipping upstream. 4. "all sections have maturity badges"

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1153:{"acceptance":"**User story:** After the upstream point release lands and DDx pulls it, `ddx agent run` with no flags just works on my machine. `--profile local`, `--harness claude`, `--model opus-4.7` behave per the upstream contract. The drain-queue failure mode (empty spec → gemini+minimax → 19-burn) is impossible.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Upstream prerequisite tagged and pulled.** Go.mod references a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class `default` and `local` profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs + gating; `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible`; public decision-trace (`RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error); cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` with unknown-cost fallback. All upstream beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` labeled `routing-plan` are closed.

2. **No DDx-side synthesis on default path.**
   - `ddx agent run` with zero flags issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call, no Model, no Harness, no Provider.
   - `ddx work` default: same shape.
   - `ddx agent execute-bead` with no overrides: same shape.
   - Integration test: same config that caused the drain-queue failure (real `~/.local/share/ddx/.ddx/config.yaml`) now either succeeds or returns a single typed error — never 19-burn.
   - Unit test: `workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker({})` → spec with `Profile: "default"`, no other fields. No `ResolveTierModelRef`, no `ResolveProfileLadder` in the call graph on the default path.

3. **Preflight before claim.**
   - Call sequence at `execute_bead_loop.go:352`: next-candidate → `ResolveRoute` → (preflight gate, new) → `Claim` → invoke. Route error OR preflight rejection exits the loop with a `worker-level` failure — no bead claimed.
   - Test: seed a pathological route (harness with allow-list that excludes the decision's model — fabricated via upstream test seam). Assert `beadStore.Claim` is never called; worker record shows `status: execution_failed` with detail naming the rejected pair; no bead has a `kind:tier-attempt` event from this attempt.

4. **Config deprecation is deliberate, not warning-only.**
   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.default_harness` set produces a **hard error** at startup with a migration pointer. Field is deleted from the config schema.
   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.profile_ladders` or `agent.routing.model_overrides` set produces a one-time warning per process AND causes the default path to ignore them. Opt-in with `--escalate` or `--override-model` explicit flags.
   - CHANGELOG entry + migration guide in DDx docs.
   - Migration test: fixture config containing all three deprecated fields, run `ddx agent run` with no flags, assert warnings printed and default path honored (not the deprecated config).

5. **Override behavior matches G3 exactly.**
   - `--harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. If claude fails upstream's gates (binary missing, quota exhausted), surfaces the typed error; no silent substitution. E2E test mocks each gate failure.
   - `--model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Lowest-cost available quota-OK provider wins. E2E test seeds 3 providers at different cost with shared model; asserts lowest-cost pick.
   - `--harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` when combo is invalid; clean dispatch otherwise.
   - `--profile local --harness claude` → upstream typed error ("claude is non-local, profile=local is local-only"); DDx surfaces it unmodified. E2E test.

6. **Playwright — drain-queue goldens.**
   - Fixture matches user's real config (endpoints: lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio, no gemini binary test or with gemini installed but not in allow-list for the chosen model).
   - Click Drain on project home → within 5s: worker row appears with harness = `agent` (or another live-endpoint-backed harness). Never gemini + non-gemini model. Never 19 failures.
   - After drain completes: worker status `success` (or typed error exit with reason recorded on the bead). Attempts count matches actual work done.

7. **No compensating DDx logic retained.**
   - Search DDx for code that re-implements any of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review.
   - `ResolveProfileLadder`, `ResolveTierModelRef`, `workersByHarness` escalation helpers are deleted from the default path. They remain reachable via `--escalate` only. Dead-code detector run in CI catches orphans.

8. **D6 / D7 are explicitly out of scope and filed as follow-ups.**
   - `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter) remains its own bead and may land before or after this one.
   - A new bead is filed post-release for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer work, pointing at A4 upstream as satisfied.

9. **Documentation.**
   - DDx's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md links to upstream profile catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract docs. No re-documentation on the DDx side.
   - DDx CHANGELOG entry covers the go.mod bump, D4 breaking changes, and new CLI surface semantics.

10. **Regression goldens for the drain-queue bug.**
    - Integration test exists that reproduces the original failure mode (empty spec + user's config + gemini binary installed + minimax model in overrides). Before this bead's work: test fails with 19-burn. After: test succeeds or exits with one clean error. Test stays in the suite as a regression guard.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:26:11.505315747Z","description":"## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

## Decision

Stopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a
multi-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it
in one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that
fails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config
schema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs
without the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.

## What is already done (verified in this worktree)

1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under
   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in
   `closed` status:
     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)
     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)
     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)
     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)
     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)

2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**
   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns
   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)
   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1
   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.

## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass

### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative

AC #1 asks for "a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class
default and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +
gating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost
dimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback."

Reading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:
  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.
  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.
  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,
    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent
    "routing point release" narrative.

The closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as
side-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the
combined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires
either (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a "v0.9.25 — routing
point release" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead
description must be relaxed to accept "the code is in v0.9.23, even if
no single CHANGELOG block summarizes it." Either resolution is a
governance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.

### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)

These criteria collectively require:
  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one
    `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call with no Model / no Harness /
    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;
  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim
    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);
  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,
    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,
    with one-time process warnings;
  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to
    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;
  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /
    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with
    dead-code detection in CI;
  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn
    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override
    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness
    conflict);
  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's
    real config;
  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.

This is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:
  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`
  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)
  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)
  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)
  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)

That work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead
explicitly bundles into "one DDx PR" — but a PR of that size needs
architectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking
config change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the
first line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC
deterministically, not to make architecture decisions.

### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads

The bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:
  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,
  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)
    stays independent.

Filing beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the
project's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is
mechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.

### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden

A net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical
19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test
(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not
have gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

To make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead
must be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:

1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever
   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add
   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially
   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.

2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default
   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:
   "default")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.

3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before
   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded
   incompatibility.

4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on
   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +
   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.

5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override
   combination.

6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test
   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.

7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of
   compensating logic.

8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.

9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI
   bead.

Each of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The
current bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one
attempt.

## Recommendation to the orchestrator

Reopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into
the nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three
(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated
goals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") suggests
this bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as
the deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.

rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

This is the second consecutive no_changes for this bead (prior attempt
20260428T222708-3b6ff345 reached the same conclusion). I re-verified the
upstream / DDx state and concur with that prior analysis: the bead is a
multi-PR epic, not a single bounded execute-bead pass, and it should be
split before another implementation attempt.

## What is verified done

1. All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are
   `closed`:
     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,
     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.

2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:
   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git merge-base --is-ancestor <SHA> v0.9.23` succeeds for the
   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked
   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary
   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no
   `go.mod` bump is even required.

## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit

The bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:

  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default
    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so
    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call —
    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,
    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.
  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before
    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.
  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes
    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`
    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema
    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.
  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests
    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and
    profile+harness conflict.
  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real
    endpoint config + a "no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback"
    assertion.
  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side
    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a
    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of
    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.
  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)
    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.
  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write
    DDx CHANGELOG entry.
  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the
    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —
    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)
    that is itself a sub-bead.

Conservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright
fixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a
deliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it
without explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files
in the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as "one
DDx PR" but the AC list is the union of nine independent
deliverables.

execute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to
make architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine
sub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC
items; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to
mechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the
shape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

Split this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior
attempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,
section "What a follow-up attempt needs"):

  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already
     satisfied by v0.9.23)
  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)
  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)
  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit
     user sign-off in its own bead description)
  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)
  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)
  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)
  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)
  9. routing-followups (AC #8)

Recommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the
split, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3
first — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") confirms
this was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive
execute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further
re-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —
the blocker is bead shape, not capability.

Attempt: 20260429T003007-e136e75e (no-op)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
================
- AC #1 is satisfied by prior work on this branch:
  * cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent at v0.9.23 (verified).
  * CHANGELOG.md (commit 7d50a792) documents the consumed upstream
    capabilities and cites each upstream commit + bead. The changelog
    explicitly notes that upstream shipped routing capabilities incrementally
    across v0.9.10..v0.9.24 rather than as a single tag.
  * All six upstream beads labeled `routing-plan` in
    ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl are CLOSED:
      agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware tiebreak)
      agent-191a74f9 (first-class default + local + standard profiles)
      agent-1f46cf22 (routing reference docs)
      agent-53f38d95 (public RouteDecision.Candidates trace)
      agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
      agent-dfabb10b (typed ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / ErrProfilePinConflict)
  * The needed public types are present in the pinned module at
    /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/agent@v0.9.23/routing_errors.go:
      ErrHarnessModelIncompatible, ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate,
      ErrUnknownProfile, ErrNoLiveProvider, DecisionWithCandidates interface
      with RouteCandidates() []RouteCandidate.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING THIS ATTEMPT
=================================
The bead is an epic-tracker, not a single deliverable. AC #2 through #10
collectively require — in one PR with no compensating paths retained — all of:

  D1. workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker rewrite + Drain-button parity.
  D2. Removal of default-path tier-ladder synthesis from
      ddx agent run / ddx agent execute-bead / ddx work; ResolveProfileLadder
      and ResolveTierModelRef gated behind --escalate only.
  D3. Preflight gate inserted before beadStore.Claim in execute_bead_loop.go
      (the bead points at line 352, which has already drifted — the actual
      Claim site is now line 341; the loop is 1052 lines and has substantial
      surrounding dispatch state to thread through).
  D4. Hard-error breaking change for `agent.routing.default_harness`;
      one-time warnings + default-path ignore for `profile_ladders` and
      `model_overrides`. Schema deletion in
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json. Config-load test fixtures.
  D5. CLI surface parity for ddx agent run (no flags / --profile / --harness /
      --model / combos), ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead.

  Plus tests demanded by the AC:
  - AC #2 integration test reproducing the original 19-burn drain failure
    against the user's real config shape.
  - AC #3 worker-level failure record assertion with seeded pathological route
    via an upstream test seam that does not currently exist on the DDx side.
  - AC #5 four end-to-end override-behavior tests (incl. cost-tiebreak
    seeded-providers test).
  - AC #6 Playwright golden for the drain-queue UI path
    (cli/internal/server/frontend); fixture must mirror user's real
    endpoint config.
  - AC #7 dead-code detector wired into CI catching orphaned ladder helpers.
  - AC #10 regression-guard integration test that fails before this work and
    succeeds after.

  Plus the directive in AC #7 — "Search DDx for code that re-implements any
  of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,
  provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review." —
  requires a deliberate audit + removal pass spanning at least:
      cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/config.go
      cli/internal/config/types.go
      cli/internal/config/routing_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json
      cli/internal/server/workers.go
      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
      cli/cmd/agent_run_profile_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (1052 lines)
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_runwithconfig_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resolvers_e2e_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_pin_propagation_test.go

This is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard
breaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be
landed responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower
sub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-
ladder removal that leaves the default path in an undefined state; a config
schema break that hard-errors existing user configs without the migration
warnings actually firing; or merging a Playwright golden whose fixture
silently passes because the underlying dispatch path was not fully rewired.

The previous attempt already correctly scoped this bead to AC #1 only and
documented the upstream prerequisite. Doing more here without
decomposition would either produce a bad commit or violate
DOWITYTD on a bead whose own description acknowledges it is a
"DDx-side tracker only" for an upstream-driven epic.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT NEEDS
==================================
Decompose ddx-fdd3ea36 into independently shippable sub-beads, each
small enough to land in one execute-bead pass with focused tests:

  ddx-<new>-D1: workerDispatchAdapter + Drain button — empty-spec defaults to
                Profile:"default"; no other synthesis. Unit test +
                dashboard wiring. Closes AC #2 partially.

  ddx-<new>-D2a: Default path stops calling ResolveTierModelRef /
                 ResolveProfileLadder. Add --escalate flag that re-enables
                 them. Tests assert call graph. Closes AC #2 fully.

  ddx-<new>-D3: Preflight gate before Claim using upstream's typed
                ErrHarnessModelIncompatible. Targeted test using upstream
                test seam (file the upstream test-seam ask as a separate
                ddx-agent bead if missing). Closes AC #3.

  ddx-<new>-D4: Config breaking-change migration. Hard-error on
                default_harness; warn-and-ignore on profile_ladders/
                model_overrides; schema update; migration fixture tests;
                CHANGELOG migration guide. Closes AC #4.

  ddx-<new>-D5: CLI flag-surface parity tests for ddx agent run / work /
                execute-bead with the four override permutations from AC #5.
                Closes AC #5.

  ddx-<new>-Audit: Audit pass for AC #7 (compensating logic removal +
                   dead-code orphan detector in CI). Closes AC #7.

  ddx-<new>-Regression: AC #10 regression-guard integration test that
                        reproduces the 19-burn drain bug; AC #2's
                        integration sibling. Closes AC #10 (and validates
                        D1+D2+D3 together).

  ddx-<new>-Playwright: AC #6 frontend golden against the rewired Drain
                        path. Last in sequence — depends on D1+D2+D3 +
                        Regression. Closes AC #6.

  ddx-<new>-Docs: Cross-link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream profile
                  catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract
                  docs (AC #9 second half — CHANGELOG half is already in
                  CHANGELOG.md from the prior commit).

  ddx-<new>-D6-followup: Decision-trace consumers in route-status /
                         providers UI — explicitly out of scope per the
                         bead description but should be filed now so the
                         tracking exists (bead description's own AC #8
                         requires this).

ddx-fdd3ea36 should remain open as the umbrella tracker until all
sub-beads close, at which point AC #2..#10 can be re-verified end-to-end
in one final review pass.

What is open (blocks tracker close)
================================================================================

              hard error on default_harness; ladders/overrides opt-in only;
              CHANGELOG migration entry. (Note: the field-removal half appears

ddx-a0cf398b  routing-followups                               open  → AC #8
              file the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer

================================================================================
Why this bead does not get implementation in this pass
================================================================================

1. The epic's AC is the union of its children's AC. Every remaining AC item
   (#4, #5, #6, #7, #8, plus the documentation half of #9) is the contract of
   a specific open child bead. Implementing them here would either duplicate
   work that the child bead will redo, or steal scope that another worker
   may already be planning to claim.

2. The bead description's "Sequencing" section explicitly orders:
     1. upstream lands (done)
     2. go.mod bump (done — ddx-967e9802)
     3. D1+D2+D3+D5 land together (D1+D2=ddx-755f5881 done; D3=ddx-98e6e9ef
        done; D5=ddx-2589e256 OPEN)
     4. D4 (ddx-87fb72c2) lands with or right after the above (OPEN)
     5. follow-ups (ddx-a0cf398b, ddx-950321ef, ddx-653f6ac9) (OPEN)
   The tracker is meant to close once steps 3–5 complete via their child
   beads, not to carry independent code.

3. Attempting any of {D4, override e2e, playwright goldens, dead-code sweep}
   in this single execute-bead pass would land a sprawling cross-cutting
   commit that violates the per-child-bead acceptance contracts and is
   hard to review. Per CLAUDE.md "DOWITYTD" and the merge-policy memory,
   the right action is to execute the children individually.

================================================================================
What a follow-up attempt should do
================================================================================

This tracker should NOT be re-queued for direct execution. Instead:

  a) Execute the four open child beads in their natural order:
        ddx-87fb72c2 (D4 — config deprecation residue + CHANGELOG)
        ddx-2589e256 (D5 — override flag e2e)
        ddx-950321ef (D6-equivalent — Playwright goldens)
        ddx-653f6ac9 (cleanup / dead-code sweep)
        ddx-a0cf398b (file follow-up beads)
     Each closes one or two AC items on this tracker.

  b) When all five close, re-open this tracker briefly to:
        - verify every AC item maps to a closed child or in-tree evidence
        - update DDx CHANGELOG (AC #9) if not already done by ddx-967e9802
        - close ddx-fdd3ea36 with a closing-comment that points at each
          child bead's closing commit.

That close step is documentation/bookkeeping — a single small commit, well
suited to a future execute-bead pass once the children are green.

No code changes were made in this pass. Workspace is clean.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1166:{"acceptance":"FEAT-002, FEAT-008, FEAT-013, FEAT-014, SD-019, and TP-002 all describe the same host+user ddx-server model, including runtime/index storage, worker boundaries, provider dashboarding, replay-backed fixtures, and mandatory Playwright coverage for new UI surfaces","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:20:09.416115777Z","description":"<context-digest>
Review area: host+user ddx-server planning stack after the server model evolved beyond the earlier multi-project topology draft. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-008, FEAT-013, FEAT-014, SD-019, TP-002, the fresh-eyes server review, and the updated requirements for a per-user host daemon, localhost HTTP UI, SQLite-backed runtime/index state, embedded-agent progress visibility, provider dashboarding, replay-backed fixtures, and Playwright coverage for every new UI surface.
</context-digest>
Update the governing server planning docs so they describe the current intended host+user ddx-server model before follow-on server design and implementation beads continue.

## Goals
- Reconcile FEAT-002, FEAT-008, FEAT-013, FEAT-014, SD-019, and TP-002 around a single host+user ddx-server instance with explicit project registry, worker management boundaries, and queue semantics
- Define the split between git-backed project truth, tracked execution evidence, and host-local runtime/index state backed by an embedded database
- Define the current transport/security model: localhost unauthenticated HTTP for UI/API now, explicit future transport/auth evolution later
- Add the embedded-agent progress, provider dashboard, replay-backed fixture, and Playwright coverage requirements to the governed server plan
- Make the scheduling boundary explicit: server may allocate workers across projects, but bead ordering remains owned by each project queue

## Boundaries
- Keep Axon and multi-host service-backed topology as later-stage work
- Do not reopen the already-shipped single-project execute-bead loop contract except where the new server plan must reference it","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"checkpointing dirty worktree: git stash: error: could not write index

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:373
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new generic `ddx bead update --unset` path deletes any key from `b.Extra`, but append-only execution evidence is also stored in `b.Extra["events"]`. In a temp bead store, `go run . bead evidence add <id> ...` followed by `go run . bead update <id> --unset events` makes `ddx bead evidence list <id> --json` return `[]`, so this patch gives normal metadata updates a data-loss path that can erase audit evidence and violate the append-only contract of `ddx bead evidence`.
Suggested fix: Restrict `--unset` to an allowlist of removable provenance fields such as `closing_commit_sha`, or explicitly reject protected keys like `events`/`session_id` and add a regression test proving evidence cannot be removed through `bead update`.","id":"hx-6a7a8bde","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: prevent bead update --unset from erasing evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-10T12:52:43.490572642Z"}

File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:6
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit fb9bdf1 closes ddx-7e88c022 with closing_commit_sha=4563fa7, an abbreviated SHA. The close store documents this field as the exact closing commit SHA when known, but manual close evidence is persisted verbatim. Replay later resolves closing_commit_sha through git rev-parse to choose the baseline commit, so storing a short SHA weakens audit and replay determinism as the repository grows and abbreviations can become ambiguous or invalid. In the current tracker, this reviewed row is one of only two abbreviated closing_commit_sha values; the durable convention elsewhere is full 40-character SHAs.
Suggested fix: Normalize ddx bead close --commit input through git rev-parse before storing it, repair ddx-7e88c022 to the full 40-character 4563fa77d88cfb0f242925a927e913841b43868c value, and add regression coverage for manual close provenance normalization.","id":"hx-83ed9550","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: normalize closing_commit_sha to full git SHAs","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:54:27.646433594Z"}

Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.651384066Z","description":"The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"sonnet","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-05-04T22:20:59.208009027Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"exit status 1

DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.880567652Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-358f2457","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-c8f79963","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-b669bb9f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-848069a3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-9228a484","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-0b8780db","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-c670ef0a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-387a0178","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"}],"description":"Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)","id":"ddx-31f745cd","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:tooling","kind:study","reliability","bead-quality"],"parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)","updated_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42.892381851Z"}

Per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 P7 ('bead = sufficient sub-agent prompt'), bead authoring quality is a dominant factor in execution success. Audit-1 (commit 86848d21) covered 20 beads; audit-2 (commit b2eb77d0) covered 108 beads; 88 of 108 scored ≤5 against the rubric. Evidence bead ddx-f339c399 documents one observed no_changes outcome (ddx-29058e2a, 2026-05-04) traced to bead-quality gap.

ROOT CAUSE
Today nothing mechanically prevents authoring a bead that's insufficient as a sub-agent prompt OR catches a non-clean execution outcome before it triggers cooldown. The 9 fail-prone layers in ddx try / ddx work (per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES) are mostly fail-closed; bead authoring quality has no observability or enforcement layer at all.

PROPOSED FRAMEWORK
A SKILL-based bead-lifecycle quality framework, layered onto the existing ddx skill (matching the bead-breakdown/ sub-skill pattern at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md). Three modes within ONE sub-skill: lint (pre-dispatch), triage (post-attempt outcome classification), refine (suggest amendments). ddx try integrates via small Go hooks that compose the existing layer-1 runner library to invoke the skill.

NO new top-level skills. NO new ddx CLI verbs. NO LLM judgment baked into Go code.

CHILD BEADS (4 sequential phases)
1. ADR-023 + FEAT amendments (governance + policy)
2. Sub-skill ships to all 5 paths + curated examples
3. ddx try hook integration (warn-only mode) + OutcomeReason field
4. Migration: close stale dups + retrofit 40 HIGH beads (during warn-only; collects baseline)

After child #4 confirms baseline acceptable false-positive rate, BLOCK mode is enabled via config (no separate bead).

NON-SCOPE
- ddx work plan quality column (defer; informational, not blocking)
- ddx bead refine standalone command (defer; refine mode in skill is invocable via ddx run)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first; package later per FEAT-015 project-local model)
- in_progress-as-eligible bug (separate concern; queue correctness, not bead quality)

GOVERNANCE
- ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md (codex correction: not 03-decide/)
- FEAT-004 amend: bead validation hooks + waiver storage + tracker metadata (FEAT-004:150 already owns bead validation hooks)
- FEAT-010 amend: ddx try / ddx work hook ordering + retry classification + outcome records
- FEAT-011 amend: nested workflow-skill packaging (FEAT-011:180 stale — says DDx ships exactly one skill, but bead-breakdown/ exists)
- FEAT-001 amend ONLY if --force --reason CLI flag added (defer to child #3 if needed)","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"sonnet","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-05-04T21:51:30.735535478Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"exit status 1

3. 'ddx try <closed-bead-id>' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead is not claimable (status=closed)'. Same shape for cancelled.

4. 'ddx try <bead-id-with-unmet-deps>' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead has unmet dependencies: <list>'.

'ddx try <ready-bead-id>' claims the bead, executes via the existing execute-bead-loop machinery (one iteration, one bead), writes evidence to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/, and exits with the bead's terminal status code (0 = success/already_satisfied; 1 = preserved_for_review/no_changes; 2 = execution_failed).

6. Flag plumbing: --harness, --model, --profile, --provider, --effort, --opaque-passthrough, --no-review, --review-harness, --review-model. Each flag, when set, plumbs identically to the corresponding 'ddx work' flag (same struct fields in the runtime config).

7. WIRED-IN: the new 'ddx try' command is registered in cli/cmd/root.go (or wherever the cobra root command assembles subcommands). Verified by 'ddx try --help' output AND by 'git grep "tryCmd\|NewTryCommand" cli/cmd/' showing the registration.

8. Tests in cli/cmd/try_test.go:
   - TestTry_BeadNotFound — invalid ID returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit
   - TestTry_BeadClosed — closed bead returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit
   - TestTry_BeadUnmetDeps — bead with unclosed dep returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit
   - TestTry_HappyPath_ClaimsAndExecutes — fixture bead + stub executor; assert bead is claimed, attempted, and outcome reported (use the existing test fixtures from agent_execute_loop_test.go as a template)

Manual verification (operator runs after merge): 'ddx try ddx-50da9674' (closed fixture-repo bead) returns non-zero with the closed-bead message; 'ddx try <some-ready-bead>' actually claims and executes it.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T20:06:45Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"463338","closing_commit_sha":"ff5e64cecc17f3094f3933986966b94a50dc4f59","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:06:37.62309311Z","description":"PROBLEM
Operators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:
- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead
- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled
- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log

This blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.

ROOT CAUSE
'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.

Code today (relevant entry points):
- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:

  ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>]

Behavior:
1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.
2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.
3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):
   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.
   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.
   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.
4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).
5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).

NON-SCOPE
- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)
- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)
- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)
- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)
- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)

INTERSECTION
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.

data.output:
ID:       ddx-06b77652
Title:    ADR-style: 7 reliability principles for ddx try / ddx work / sub-agent execution
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, area:agent, area:server, kind:design, reliability, bead-quality
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Desc:     The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads
Accept:   1. docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md exists and contains all 7 principles with their statements above (verbatim).
2. README sections of each refactor child (C4, C5, C6, C7, C8, C9, C11, C12, C13) cite the principle(s) the child enforces. Cross-link in those bead descriptions added via ddx bead update.
3. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md updated with one-line pointers: 'See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.'
4. cd cli && go test ./... still green (no code change; sanity check that nothing broke).
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
6. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].
